mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 cash flows and generating positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized strength in consumer purchasing power and construction sector demand, which amplifies corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: An upward revision in the index of industrial production reflects immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which triggers short-term momentum buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases are followed by negative 5-day forward returns in over 60% of observations during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 220.0, it indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy, increasing equity valuation multiples compression and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL remains above 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, leading to sustained positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of increasing PAYEMS and decreasing UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC for 60-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect broad-based economic expansion that improves corporate profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a cumulative abnormal return indistinguishable from zero across all quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 90.0, it signals a severe contraction in manufacturing and industrial activity that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where INDPRO drops below 90.0.
